item.text: I’m going to locate the trait definition strictly through Orbit graph commands, then verify the exact file path, owning crate, and signature line with direct command output. I’ll start by discovering which `orbit.graph.*` query exposes symbol definitions.

Execute a tool

Usage: orbit tool run [OPTIONS] <NAME>

Arguments:
  <NAME>  Tool name

Options:
      --input <INPUT>            JSON input for the tool (use --input-file to avoid shell escaping issues with rich content)
      --root <ROOT>              Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
      --input-file <INPUT_FILE>  Path to a JSON file to use as input (bypasses shell escaping; preferred for markdown or multi-line content)
      --agent <AGENT>            Explicit agent name for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_NAME)
      --model <MODEL>            Explicit agent model for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_MODEL)
      --timeout <TIMEOUT>        Execution timeout (e.g. "30s", "5000ms")
      --dry-run                  Validate without executing
      --fields <FIELDS>          Comma-separated top-level fields to keep from object output
      --full                     Return the tool's full unfiltered JSON output
      --pretty                   Pretty-print JSON output for human debugging
      --output <OUTPUT>          Output format [default: json] [possible values: json, text]
  -h, --help                     Print help

Manage and run Orbit tools, including external MCP plugins

  list      List all registered tools

item.text: I’ve confirmed `orbit tool` supports listing and direct execution. Next I’m narrowing to the exact `orbit.graph.*` tool names so we can query the symbol definition without touching file-search commands.

item.text: I hit a read-only DB error on `orbit tool show`, so I’m switching to direct `orbit tool run orbit.graph.*` calls with minimal JSON to discover accepted input shape and keep moving within your benchmark constraints.

The graph search found a single `trait` symbol named `V2RuntimeHost`; I’m now resolving that symbol with `orbit.graph.show` to extract the exact `pub trait ...` declaration line and confirm it’s a definition.
I’ve extracted the exact trait block and line range from graph output. I’m now running one more graph query to validate crate ownership explicitly so the crate name is evidence-backed too.

- Trait definition file (workspace-relative): [crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/dispatcher.rs](/Users/daniel/workspace/repos/orbit/crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/dispatcher.rs)
- Owning crate: `orbit-engine`
- Signature line (verbatim): `pub trait V2RuntimeHost: Send + Sync {`
